summary: Historical Kommunalwahlen turnout: 2008=59.5%, 2014=54.7% (historic low), 2020=58.7% (during COVID). Long decline from >80% in 1950s. Recent Bavaria engagement: Landtag 2023=73.1%, Bundestag 2025=84.5%. Central estimate for 2026: ~60%.
content:
Historical Bavarian Kommunalwahlen statewide turnout:
- 1950s: >80%
- 2008: 59.5%
- 2014: 54.7% (described as historical low)
- 2020: 58.7% (during COVID pandemic)

Key factors for 2026:
- No COVID disruption (positive)
- Higher general political engagement in Bavaria (Landtag 2023: 73.1%, Bundestag 2025: 84.5%)
- Growing Briefwahl (mail voting) infrastructure
- Kommunalwahlen structurally lower turnout than state/federal
- Per-cycle volatility ~4-5 pp based on 3 data points

Monte Carlo central estimate: ~60.2% with std ~3.5 pp
